sourceMovie.title: Big Fish
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Big Fish (2003) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tim Burton's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Where the Wild Things Are
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Big Fish clicked for you, Where the Wild Things Are (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Wizard of Oz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Big Fish clicked for you, The Wizard of Oz (1939) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Big Fish clicked for you, The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hook
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Big Fish clicked for you, Hook (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Big Fish?
faq.answer: Try Where the Wild Things Are, The Wizard of Oz, The Chronicles of Narnia: The Voyage of the Dawn Treader—each shares DNA with Big Fish while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Big Fish?
faq.answer: Big Fish (2003) is best described as Adventure, Fantasy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Big Fish worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
